mechanism_narrative: Sustained elevation of the federal funds rate above 3.5% signals restrictive monetary policy that increases discount rates on equity valuations and reduces corporate profitability through higher borrowing costs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 3.78% indicates restrictive stance
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 3.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets reprrice growth.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 50 thousand persons in single month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances following PAYEMS declines > 50k, or correlation between PAYEMS momentum and 5-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained decline in real retail sales and food services indicates weakening consumer demand that reduces corporate revenue growth expectations. Combined with elevated unemployment rate above 4%, this signals demand-side recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 689443 million dollars, UNRATE at 3.7%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the joint effect of RSAFS and UNRATE on 60-day returns is not statistically significant (interaction p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained expansion in industrial production index above 104 signals robust supply-side capacity utilization and productivity growth that supports corporate profit margins and earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO and 20-day forward returns is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative returns in > 55% of periods when INDPRO is above 104.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts measured by HOUST interact with monetary policy (FEDFUNDS): when HOUST declines > 10% month-over-month during periods of FEDFUNDS > 3.5%, this signals credit-constrained housing demand and forward-looking recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 1425 thousand units, FEDFUNDS at 3.78%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term between HOUST momentum and FEDFUNDS level is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in predicting 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: Acceleration of consumer price inflation measured by CPIAUCSL above 3% month-over-month signals persistent inflation that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain or increase restrictive policy, constraining equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced real earnings growth over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances when CPIAUCSL monthly change > 0.3%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate remains below 4% while payroll employment growth continues, signaling tight labor market conditions that support consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as markets price sustained demand.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 3.7%, PAYEMS at 153548 thousand
regime_hint: tight labor market with sustained employment growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the joint effect of low UNRATE and positive PAYEMS growth on 5-day returns is not statistically significant (interaction p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured by GDPC1 decelerates below 2% annualized rate, signaling late-cycle slowdown that reduces forward earnings expectations and equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as markets reprice growth and recession probability.
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 20039.406 billion chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances when GDPC1 annualized growth < 2%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window during low-growth periods.
